Approach

My approach to your AI challenge

Done on the plant floor, not in a lab. Here is how I would attack it.

1. Start where the P&L is

I target the money first: inventory and working capital, service and on-time delivery, expedite cost and the financial close. AI has to move those numbers to earn its place.

2. Govern it so it scales safely

Earned value, human-in-the-loop review, an agent registry, security and a cap on AI run cost. Agents that are controlled, auditable and predictable, not a science project.

3. Prove value in 90 days

A live control tower and outcome tracking from week one, so you see results and risk in real time and decide on evidence, not at the end.

Built to de-risk AI. Governance and human-in-the-loop, an agent registry, AI run-cost caps tracked against forecast, security and compliance from a patented background, and the adoption and change management that makes it stick.

Positioning

Two strengths, one operator

Well planned is good. Delivered is better.

Enterprise AI, applied

Certified in Oracle AI Agent Studio. I have built and deployed AI agents for inventory reconciliation, supply-chain orchestration and finance automation, and stood up a real-time AI operations control tower across ERP, WMS and MES. Program leadership, governed

Five Oracle Fusion go-lives in five years, delivered on time and within budget. I run programs on earned value management with >97% budget accuracy and >90% milestone compliance, chair executive steering committees and govern multi-vendor SI teams of 25 to 60 across the Americas, EMEA and LATAM.

AI-Enabled Supply Chain Governance

−40% mismatches

Established an AI-driven control framework for inventory reconciliation and transfer orchestration, embedding governance, automation and data integrity into core supply-chain processes at a $1B CPG manufacturer.

  • Built AI agents in Oracle AI Agent Studio with human-in-the-loop reconciliation workflows.
  • Achieved a 40% reduction in cross-system mismatches between WMS and Oracle Fusion.
  • Improved fulfillment precision and customer service reliability through cleaner governed data.

Network Security Devices & SaaS Platform

Patented · Exited

At Minestream Software I led technology strategy and global development for a line of stealthy reverse-firewall network devices that stopped information leaks at the network door, paired with a SaaS dashboard that surfaced security violations in real time.

SaaS dashboard surfaced security policy violations and information leaks at the network doorstep, through a secure browser interface.
The packet collector ran on a hand-held device, deployable anywhere on the network.
  • Led the design of stealthy reverse-firewall network devices that stopped information leaks at the network door, working in tandem with a SaaS-based dashboard.
  • Architected an embedded-technology product strategy for a suite of internet safety devices, and built four consumer network-security appliances in 24 months.
  • Named on an international technology patent featuring 65 claims, creating strategic advantage and an exit path in a competitive market.
  • Managed global development and operations for an international engineering team across the U.S. and offshore.
  • Cultivated device-manufacturer partnerships across the U.S., Israel and Taiwan that drove software performance, functionality and value.
  • Oversaw security strategy consulting and integration on the highest-profile, multi-million-dollar accounts.

How I run KPI & outcome management

Define

Baseline each outcome and tie it to a dollar lever such as working capital, expedite avoidance, labor productivity or margin protection.

Govern

Track CPI, SPI and stage-gate criteria in a control tower, reviewed with steering committees on a fixed cadence.

Realize

Convert KPI movement into realized value, with incentive pools and stage gates keeping delivery honest.
